hero.sub: Bookings, POS, accounting, CRM, inventory, and a branded client portal — bilingual AR + EN, ZATCA & ETA ready, deployable on your own server in under an hour.
hero.subStrong: Pay once. Host it anywhere. No per-user fees, ever.

why.body: Most booking platforms grow more expensive as you grow. Momentfy goes the other way — one payment, unlimited everything, and every line of the source is yours to change.

items.title: Source you own
items.body: Every file. Every query. Every pixel. Change what you need, build what's missing — and none of it ever expires.

items.title: Self-hosted, SaaS-ready
items.body: Run it as your in-house system — or enable multi-tenant mode and resell it to thousands of businesses.

items.title: Bilingual, RTL-native

items.title: Compliance built-in

items.title: Built-in AI assistant

items.title: Installs like a native app
items.body: Progressive web app on iOS and Android — no app store review. One build runs on web, tablet and phone.

zatca.long: Saudi Arabia's ZATCA Phase 2 e-invoicing rules require invoices to be cryptographically signed, QR-stamped, and submitted to the Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority in real time. Momentfy includes the complete flow — CSR, CSID issuance, UBL signing, clearance, and reporting — without relying on an external SaaS layer.
zatca.whoFor: Any Saudi-registered business with a Tax Registration Number issuing VAT invoices.
setupSteps: Enter your TRN on the tenant settings page
Generate a CSR in the admin and upload it to the ZATCA Fatoora portal
Paste the CSID response back into Momentfy
Test in sandbox, then flip to production

sections.heading: CSR & CSID issuance
sections.body: Generate a Certificate Signing Request directly from the admin panel and submit it to ZATCA's Fatoora compliance endpoint. Paste the returned CSID back into Momentfy to authorise the device — sandbox certificates for testing, production for live clearance, switched with a single toggle.

sections.heading: UBL 2.1 signing on your server
sections.body: Every invoice is built as a UBL 2.1 XML document, hashed with SHA-256, signed and stamped on your own server using the device private key. The keys never leave your infrastructure — no SaaS signer, no per-invoice fee, no vendor in the cryptographic chain.

sections.heading: QR stamp on every receipt
sections.body: A TLV-encoded QR carrying seller name, VAT number, timestamp, total and VAT amount is stamped on every printed thermal receipt, A4 invoice and PDF download. The code is generated locally, so it works even when the connection to ZATCA is down.

sections.heading: B2B clearance & B2C reporting
sections.body: B2B standard invoices flow through real-time clearance — the invoice is held until ZATCA returns the cleared XML and stamp. B2C simplified invoices use the reporting flow — printed instantly with the QR, then submitted within 24 hours per ZATCA rules.

sections.heading: Hourly retry queue
sections.body: Failed or pending submissions land in a queue that an hourly cron re-processes automatically. Each retry records the error code and ZATCA response so support can audit exactly why a particular invoice was rejected.

sections.heading: Cleared-invoice archival
sections.body: Cleared invoices are archived alongside the signed XML, the SHA-256 hash, the cryptographic stamp and the ZATCA UUID. Auditors get the full chain of custody — issuance, clearance, retention — straight from the database.

sections.heading: Saudi Riyal glyph (U+20C1)
sections.body: The new SAR symbol is bundled as a custom font and rendered correctly on every receipt, invoice and PDF — including thermal printers that don't carry it natively. Receipts print compliant from the day you flip the switch.

eta.long: Egypt's ETA (Egyptian Tax Authority) requires every B2B invoice to be mapped to item and customer codes before it is submitted to the authority portal. Momentfy builds the full ETA document structure, signs and submits it, tracks status per invoice, and resubmits rejections automatically.
eta.whoFor: Any Egypt-registered business required to submit ETA e-invoices.
setupSteps: Obtain your ETA client secret and upload to tenant settings
Map tax rates to ETA tax codes via the settings table
Map top-level SKUs to ETA item codes (CSV upload)
Submit a test invoice, verify acceptance, go live

sections.heading: Item & customer code mapping
sections.body: Map each tax rate, SKU and customer to the corresponding ETA codes in tenant settings — once per item, once per customer. The mapping table accepts CSV bulk-upload so you don't recode the catalogue by hand.

sections.heading: End-to-end document builder
sections.body: Momentfy builds the full ETA JSON document — header, lines, tax breakdown, payment terms — straight from the invoice you already created. No external middleware, no second portal to keep in sync.

sections.heading: Signed submission with status
sections.body: Each invoice is signed with your ETA client secret and submitted to the authority portal. Status flows live on the invoice — submitted → accepted → cleared — with the UUID and long-id retained for accounting exports.

sections.heading: Credit & debit notes
sections.body: Credit (c) and debit (d) note types are supported via ETA's document-type flag, with the original invoice referenced for audit. Reverse a sale and the ETA submission flows automatically.

sections.heading: UUID, long-id & status retained
sections.body: Every ETA-tracked invoice keeps the UUID, long-id, internal-id and submission status on the record itself — queryable per invoice, exportable for the accountant, defensible during audit.

sections.heading: Auto-resubmission on failure
sections.body: Transient ETA API failures trigger an automatic retry with exponential backoff. Permanent rejections stay on the invoice with the error message intact, so you can correct and resubmit without losing the trail.

ai.long: An AI assistant built on the Vercel AI SDK for the client booking flow and branded portal. Bring any OpenAI-compatible endpoint — OpenAI, Gemini, Groq, DeepSeek, x.ai, Together, or a local Ollama / LM Studio model. The assistant is grounded in your services, opening hours, prices and policies so it guides bookings without inventing availability.
ai.whoFor: Any business that wants a 24/7 assistant without hiring extra receptionists.
setupSteps: Enable the AI Assistant add-on from tenant settings
Paste your OpenAI-compatible API key and endpoint URL
Set the brand system-prompt suffix, dialect and tone
Test in the booking site preview, then publish

sections.heading: In-app chat widget
sections.body: Drop-in chat surface on the public booking site and inside the branded client portal. Clients ask questions in their own language and get answers grounded in your real services and policies — not the assistant's training data.

sections.heading: Grounded on real availability
sections.body: The assistant reads from your live calendar, services, staff schedule and price list. It will never offer a slot you can't deliver, a service you don't sell or a price you didn't set.

sections.heading: Books appointments end-to-end
sections.body: The assistant guides clients through service selection, staff preference, date and time — and creates a confirmed appointment when the client agrees. The handoff to your calendar is real, not a lead form.

sections.heading: Brand voice & dialect per tenant
sections.body: A per-tenant system-prompt suffix lets you set tone (warm / professional / playful), dialect (Gulf, Egyptian, MSA, English) and brand-specific phrasing. The assistant sounds like your business, not like a generic chatbot.

sections.heading: Any OpenAI-compatible provider
sections.body: Pick your provider per tenant — OpenAI, Google Gemini, Groq, DeepSeek, x.ai, Together, or a local Ollama / LM Studio model. The Vercel AI SDK abstracts the wire format so swapping providers is a settings change, not a deploy.

sections.heading: Bring-your-own API key
sections.body: Requests go directly from your server to the provider you chose. No mark-up, no rate-limiting through us, and your token usage and bill stay between you and the provider.

gcal.long: Two-way sync between each staff member's Google Calendar and their Momentfy schedule. Appointments booked in Momentfy appear on their Google Calendar instantly; busy blocks from Google are pulled in so nobody gets double-booked.
gcal.whoFor: Teams where staff already live in Google Calendar for personal commitments.
setupSteps: Enable the Google Calendar add-on
Create a Google Cloud project and OAuth client (one-time)
Paste client ID / secret into tenant settings
Each staff member clicks 'Connect my calendar' on their profile

sections.heading: Per-staff OAuth
sections.body: Each team member authorises their own Google account from their staff profile — the tenant doesn't share a single connection across the team. Revoking access for one member doesn't break sync for the others.

sections.heading: Outbound: Momentfy → Google
sections.body: Appointments booked, edited or cancelled in Momentfy push to the assigned staff member’s Google Calendar within seconds. The event includes the client name, service, location and a deep link back to the appointment in Momentfy.

sections.heading: Inbound: Google → blocked time
sections.body: Busy blocks from the staff member’s Google Calendar are pulled in as blocked-time on the Momentfy schedule. Personal commitments, school runs and holidays automatically protect availability without anyone keying them in twice.

sections.heading: Timezone-aware sync
sections.body: The sync respects the tenant timezone, the staff timezone and Google’s recurring-event quirks. DST transitions, traveling staff and cross-timezone clients are handled cleanly.

sections.heading: Re-auth from staff profile
sections.body: If a staff member revokes access — or Google expires the token — a clear re-auth banner appears on their profile. Reconnect in two clicks; sync resumes automatically and missed events backfill.

sections.heading: Personal & Workspace accounts
sections.body: Works equally well with free Gmail accounts and Google Workspace. Permissions, scopes and incremental authorisation are handled the same way for either account type.

insurance.long: Insurance classes, coverage rules, and claim-ready invoices for clinics that split a single visit between patient and insurer. Every service can have per-class coverage percentages, co-pays, and visit caps.
insurance.whoFor: Dental, medical and aesthetics clinics with mixed-insurance patients.
setupSteps: Create insurance classes under Catalogue → Insurance
Define coverage rules per service (percentage + cap)
Assign each patient's insurance class in their profile
Book a visit — co-pay is calculated automatically at POS

sections.heading: Tiered insurance classes
sections.body: Define unlimited insurance classes — BUPA Gold, Tawuniya Silver, Allianz Plus — each with its own coverage profile. Classes are reusable across patients and can be edited centrally without touching individual records.

sections.heading: Per-service coverage rules
sections.body: Set coverage percentage per service per class, with optional per-visit caps and annual ceilings. Cosmetic services can be excluded outright; specialist procedures can require pre-approval.

sections.heading: Auto co-pay at POS
sections.body: Co-pay is calculated at checkout based on the patient's class, the service rules and any deductible already met this period. Reception sees the patient share, the insurer share and the visit total without lifting a calculator.

sections.heading: Claim-ready invoice exports
sections.body: Each invoice exports a claim package with the insurer-share line, patient-share line, service codes and supporting documents. The format is ready for the insurer portal — or the printable claim form, if that is what your provider still wants.

sections.heading: Patient policy on profile
sections.body: Each client carries their insurance class, policy number and validity period on their profile. Expired policies surface as a warning at checkout so reception verifies before serving — never after.

sections.heading: Claims dashboard
sections.body: A dedicated dashboard shows claims volume by class, by provider and by status — submitted, paid, partially paid, rejected. Aging tells you which insurers are dragging payment so you can apply pressure where it matters.

dental.long: Interactive odontogram for adult and pediatric patients with surface-level tracking. Click any tooth (or surface) to log caries, fillings, crowns, root canals, bridges, veneers or sealants. Each tooth links to a medical record for full audit.
setupSteps: Enable the Dental Chart add-on
Select adult or pediatric dentition when opening a chart
Open any patient's profile — the chart tab appears
Click teeth to log procedures during or after the visit

sections.heading: Adult & pediatric dentition
sections.body: FDI numbering with full adult dentition (32 teeth) and pediatric dentition (20 teeth), switchable per chart. The chart auto-detects the right layout based on the patient age but can be overridden case-by-case.

sections.heading: 5-surface tracking per tooth
sections.body: Every tooth supports the five clinical surfaces — mesial, distal, occlusal, buccal and lingual — recorded individually. A two-surface filling on tooth 16 reads exactly as the dentist documented it, not as a generic mark.

sections.heading: Color-coded conditions
sections.body: Caries, fillings, crowns, missing teeth, implants, root canals, bridges, veneers and sealants each render in a distinct colour on the chart. A glance reveals the patient’s history before the dentist reads a single note.

sections.heading: Surface-level notes
sections.body: Attach a note to any specific surface of any specific tooth — material used, shade, follow-up needed. Notes travel with the chart between visits and surface in the timeline view.

sections.heading: Tied to the medical record
sections.body: Every chart change is linked to a medical-records entry, complete with the timestamp, the practitioner and the visit. The chart becomes legally defensible documentation, not just a visual aid.

sections.heading: Printable & PDF export
sections.body: Export the chart as a print-ready PDF on the clinic letterhead — for referrals, second opinions, insurance claims or the patient's own records.

imaging.long: A built-in DICOM viewer so X-rays, panoramics, and intra-oral images live inside the patient's file — not on a separate computer. Upload a DICOM, view it with proper window/level/zoom/pan, annotate, and attach to a visit.
imaging.whoFor: Dental and medical clinics that generate DICOM imaging (X-ray, CBCT, ultrasound).
setupSteps: Enable the Clinical Imaging add-on
Configure the S3 bucket where DICOM files are stored
Upload images from the patient's profile
Annotate and attach to the relevant visit

sections.heading: DICOM parser built-in
sections.body: Accepts standard .dcm files generated by intra-oral sensors, panoramic units, CBCT scanners and ultrasound. Pixel data, window/level, modality and patient header are parsed via dicom-parser and rendered in the browser.

sections.heading: Window / level / zoom / pan
sections.body: Full diagnostic viewer controls — adjust window/level for soft-tissue vs. bone contrast, zoom for fine caries detection, pan around large CBCT volumes. Keyboard shortcuts and touch gestures both work.

sections.heading: Annotations toolkit
sections.body: Mark up images with arrows, circles, rectangles, text labels, distance and angle measurements, and freehand drawing. Annotations save with the image and travel into the patient timeline.

sections.heading: Linked to the visit
sections.body: Each image is attached to a specific patient visit and shows up alongside the SOAP note and prescription on that day. The visit is the unit of clinical truth — not a folder of orphaned files.

sections.heading: Before/after pairing
sections.body: Pair pre-treatment and post-treatment images for visual comparison — orthodontic progress, restorative work, surgical outcomes. The paired view is one-click for case presentation or insurance submission.

sections.heading: S3-isolated storage
sections.body: DICOM files are stored on S3-compatible object storage with strict per-tenant isolation. Thumbnails are generated on upload for fast portal access on mobile, and the full-resolution image is fetched on demand.

labs.long: Order clinical lab tests from a visit, receive results back (CSV, PDF, or manually entered), and optionally publish them to the patient's portal. Ships with a configurable lab catalogue and reference-range validation.
labs.whoFor: Clinics that order blood work, microbiology, or path tests through external labs.
setupSteps: Enable the Lab Orders add-on
Import the lab catalogue CSV (or build one in the admin)
Order a test during any visit
Enter or upload the result when it arrives

sections.heading: Structured test catalogue
sections.body: Build or import a lab catalogue with test name, units, normal-range minimum and maximum and gender/age modifiers. Reference ranges drive the auto-flagging when results come back.

sections.heading: Order from a visit
sections.body: Click "Order labs" from any visit — pick tests from the catalogue, add notes for the lab, and the order is generated with patient demographics already attached. The visit timeline shows the order alongside notes and prescriptions.

sections.heading: Status workflow
sections.body: A clear pipeline — ordered → sent-to-lab → results-received → reviewed-by-clinician — drives the queue. Reception, lab tech and clinician each act on the queue from their own dashboard.

sections.heading: Structured + flagged results
sections.body: Results are captured as structured numeric values (with units) and free text where needed. Out-of-range values auto-flag as HIGH, LOW or ABNORMAL based on the catalogue’s reference range.

sections.heading: PDFs & file attachments
sections.body: External lab reports — PDFs, scanned forms, raw analyser exports — attach to the order and stay linked to the patient's record forever. No more chasing emails for last quarter's CBC.

sections.heading: Portal publishing
sections.body: Publish reviewed results to the client portal with a single click, with abnormal values highlighted clearly. Clients see what their doctor wants them to see — nothing accidental, nothing premature.

rx.long: Author prescriptions during the visit and download or print them on a branded prescription pad. Patients can access their full prescription history in the client portal.
rx.whoFor: Any clinic that prescribes — GPs, dentists, specialists.
setupSteps: Enable the Prescriptions add-on
Upload your prescription-pad letterhead (optional)
Build or import a drug catalogue
Write a prescription during any visit

sections.heading: Drug catalogue with presets
sections.body: Build a catalogue of medications with default dosage, frequency, duration and units per drug. Common prescriptions become two-click entries instead of free-text writing every time.

sections.heading: Multi-item prescriptions
sections.body: Compose a prescription with multiple items, each with its own dose, frequency, duration and quantity. The print layout follows the conventional Rx pad format clinicians and pharmacists already read at a glance.

sections.heading: Branded prescription pad
sections.body: Print on the clinic letterhead — your logo, doctor name, license number, signature placeholder and the auto-generated prescription number. PDF download for cases where printing is offsite.

sections.heading: Tied to patient & visit
sections.body: Every prescription anchors to the specific patient and the specific visit, with prescriber and timestamp recorded. The patient timeline shows the full prescribing history in one place.

sections.heading: Auto-numbered for traceability
sections.body: A monotonic prescription number is generated at issue, retained on print copies and surfaced in audit logs. Lost prescriptions are reissued with a new number and the old one is voided cleanly.

sections.heading: Patient access in portal
sections.body: Patients can view their full prescription history in the branded client portal — pull up the previous Rx, share it with a pharmacy, or screenshot it for travel. The clinic's tenant copy stays authoritative.

records.long: Structured electronic medical records (EMR) with SOAP-style note templates, vitals logging, and allergy/diagnosis tracking. Each visit lives on the patient timeline with full attachment support.
records.whoFor: Clinics that want a real EMR without paying for a separate system.
setupSteps: Enable the Medical Records add-on
Choose or create SOAP templates for your specialties
Start writing notes during or after each visit
Tag allergies and diagnoses in the patient profile

sections.heading: SOAP-style note structure
sections.body: Each note follows the Subjective / Objective / Assessment / Plan structure clinicians actually use — chief complaint, examination findings, diagnosis with codes and treatment plan. Templates speed up routine visit types without removing flexibility.

sections.heading: Custom templates per specialty
sections.body: Build templates per specialty — pediatric well-visit, orthodontic adjustment, dermatology consult — and pre-fill the structure on every new visit. Templates are tenant-owned and editable as the practice evolves.

sections.heading: Vitals as structured fields
sections.body: Capture blood pressure, heart rate, temperature, respiration, weight and height as numeric fields, not free text. Trends graph automatically across visits — pediatric growth charts, hypertension monitoring, weight management.

sections.heading: Allergies & ICD-10 diagnoses
sections.body: Allergies are flagged on the patient header so they're visible the moment any clinician opens the chart. Diagnoses carry an ICD-10 code field for insurance claims and structured reporting.

sections.heading: Attachments anywhere
sections.body: Attach images, PDFs, scanned forms and external reports to any visit — and link to DICOM clinical images and lab orders without re-uploading. Each attachment stays with the visit it belongs to.

sections.heading: Patient timeline view
sections.body: The patient's entire history rolls up into a single chronological timeline — every visit, prescription, lab order, image and SOAP note in one scroll. Onboarding a new clinician to a complex case takes minutes, not an afternoon.

resources.long: Book rooms, chairs, equipment, or any physical asset alongside staff. A haircut needs a chair; a root canal needs a chair + hygienist; a class needs a room. Resources make all of this bookable and conflict-aware.
resources.whoFor: Any business where the physical asset matters as much as the staff.
setupSteps: Enable the Resources add-on
Define resource types per location
Attach resource requirements to each service in the catalogue
Book normally — conflicts are caught automatically

sections.heading: Resource types per location
sections.body: Define resource types per location — chairs, dental units, treatment rooms, ultrasound machines, photography studios. Quantity is tracked per type per branch so capacity reflects reality.

sections.heading: Required-resource rules
sections.body: Each service can declare which resource types it needs — a haircut needs a chair, a root canal needs a chair plus a hygienist station, a yoga class needs a studio. Booking enforces the rule automatically.

sections.heading: Conflict-aware booking
sections.body: When a service is booked, both the staff calendar and the assigned resource are checked. The system refuses double-bookings even when the staff member is technically free, because the chair isn’t.

sections.heading: Maintenance & downtime blocks
sections.body: Block a resource for cleaning, maintenance or downtime — with a reason field and a duration. Blocked resources disappear from booking availability until the block clears.

sections.heading: Tied to appointment record
sections.body: Each appointment carries the resource reservation alongside the staff assignment, so the audit trail shows exactly which chair, room or machine was used and when.

sections.heading: Capacity feeds availability
sections.body: The booking site's slot calculation accounts for resource capacity per location. If you have three chairs and three are full at 2pm, 2pm is unavailable — even when the next stylist is free.

attendance.long: Track client check-ins for gyms, schools, and any recurring program where attendance matters more than booking a time slot. Includes a kiosk-friendly check-in flow, per-client history and NO_SHOW capture.
attendance.whoFor: Gyms, yoga studios, schools, martial-arts clubs, tutoring centres.
setupSteps: Enable the Client Attendance add-on
Configure the kiosk (tablet + wall mount) or client-app check-in
Assign check-in credits to each membership
Let clients check themselves in

sections.heading: One-tap check-in
sections.body: Clients check in via their phone, a kiosk tablet at the door, or a QR code on the wall. The flow is designed for speed — under three seconds from arrival to recorded attendance.

sections.heading: Per-client attendance history
sections.body: Every check-in is linked to the appointment, the membership and the client record. The client's attendance history is queryable by date range, by class, by membership cycle.

sections.heading: NO_SHOW capture
sections.body: Booked clients who never check in are automatically flagged as NO_SHOW after the appointment window closes. Charge fees, deduct credits or trigger a follow-up message based on tenant policy.

sections.heading: Stale-appointment resolver
sections.body: A nightly job sweeps any appointment that finished without a check-in or a manual completion and resolves it to either COMPLETED or NO_SHOW based on tenant rules. The schedule never carries old open appointments forward.

sections.heading: Per-membership attendance counts
sections.body: Memberships with 'unlimited' or 'N visits per month' rules tally check-ins automatically. The client portal shows visits used and visits remaining; reception sees the same number at the door.

sections.heading: API for HR / school audits
sections.body: Attendance records are queryable via the platform API — useful for schools that need term-end attendance reports, gyms that audit corporate-wellness contracts, and tutoring centres that bill on session counts.

loyalty.long: Points-based loyalty with earning rules, redemptions, expiry, and a wallet view in the client portal. Turns one-time customers into recurring ones without discounting every visit.
loyalty.whoFor: Any business where repeat visits matter — salons, cafés, clinics.
setupSteps: Enable the Loyalty Points add-on
Define earn rules in the catalogue (points per service or per unit spent)
Set redemption rules (minimum, expiry, eligible services)
Launch — clients accumulate points automatically on every sale

sections.heading: Earn rules per tenant
sections.body: Configure earning rules per service or per unit spent — flat points per visit, multipliers for retail, bonuses for first-time visits. Rules are tenant-scoped and live, so you can re-balance the program without a re-deploy.

sections.heading: Redemption rules
sections.body: Set a minimum balance before redemption is possible, an expiry window per earned points batch, and which services or products are eligible for redemption. The cashier sees what's redeemable at checkout — no manual look-up.

sections.heading: Auto-expiry with notification
sections.body: Points expire on a per-tenant schedule. Clients receive an in-portal warning and a configurable channel notification before lapse, so dormant clients return to spend before they lose what they earned.

sections.heading: Wallet view in client portal
sections.body: Clients see their balance, full earning history, redemption ledger and expiring-point breakdown inside their branded portal. Transparency builds trust and removes "what happened to my points?" support tickets.

sections.heading: Targetable segments
sections.body: Top spenders, dormant high-value clients and lapsing-points clients are pre-built segments — ready for campaigns, ready for reactivation flows, ready to reward in person at the next visit.

sections.heading: Stats endpoint & ledger export
sections.body: Loyalty performance — issuance rate, redemption rate, expiry rate, ROI by segment — is exposed via API and exportable to CSV. The loyalty manager can prove the program’s impact, not just hope for it.

events.long: Group classes, workshops, and scheduled events with capacity, waitlists, and attendee check-in. A yoga class at 7am with 12 seats — Momentfy sells them, confirms them, and checks them in.
events.whoFor: Fitness studios, cooking schools, workshop hosts, community centres.
setupSteps: Enable the Events & Classes add-on
Create event templates (Yoga 60, HIIT 45, etc.)
Schedule recurring slots in the calendar
Publish to the booking site — clients start signing up

sections.heading: One-off & recurring events
sections.body: Create a one-time workshop or schedule a recurring class with capacity, ticket types and pricing. Each occurrence is a separate bookable event with its own seat count, so cancellations and waitlists work per session.

sections.heading: Capacity & waitlist
sections.body: Sold-out events automatically open a waitlist with position tracking. As cancellations free up seats, the next person on the list is notified and given a configurable window to confirm before the seat moves on.

sections.heading: Per-ticket QR & check-in
sections.body: Each ticket carries its own QR code. Door staff check attendees in via a kiosk or staff phone — under two seconds per ticket — and the count of attendees-on-site is live for the instructor's clipboard.

sections.heading: Tiered ticket pricing
sections.body: Configure ticket tiers per event — free, fixed price, member-only, early-bird. Tiers carry their own capacity caps so you can sell 10 early-bird seats and 20 standard ones from the same event.

sections.heading: Sales & ledger integration
sections.body: Tickets flow through the sales system — invoiced, taxed, ZATCA-cleared where applicable — and revenue lands in the right ledger account automatically. Refunds for cancellations create credit notes the same way.

sections.heading: Live counts on every listing
sections.body: Tickets-sold, available-spots and waitlist length update on the public event page in real time. The page never offers a seat that has just been claimed elsewhere.

followup.long: An automated queue of follow-up tasks so nothing slips through. 'Call Sarah about her post-op checkup.' 'Ask Karim for a review after his third visit.' Rules generate the tasks; assigned staff close them.
followup.whoFor: Any operator who says "we forgot to call them back".
setupSteps: Enable the Follow-up Tasks add-on
Create rules (e.g. 'call 2 days after first visit')
Assign default owners per task type
Staff process the queue from their dashboard

sections.heading: Rule-driven generation
sections.body: Define rules — "call 2 days after first visit", "follow up post-op on day 7", "ask for a review after the third visit" — and the system generates the tasks automatically. Rules accept relative dates, segment filters and channel preferences.

sections.heading: Assigned with SLA
sections.body: Every task is assigned to a specific staff member with a due date. Overdue tasks surface on the team dashboard so nothing rots in someone’s queue unnoticed.

sections.heading: Status & outcome capture
sections.body: When a staff member closes a task, they record the outcome — answered, voicemail, rescheduled, no-interest. Outcomes feed the analytics and inform whether the rule itself is working.

sections.heading: Snooze with reason
sections.body: Tasks can be snoozed to a later date with a reason — patient was in surgery, client was abroad, line was busy. Snoozes preserve the trail and keep the next attempt visible without filling the daily queue.

sections.heading: Bulk batch close
sections.body: Mark a batch of completed calls in one action — "all called, all answered, all rebooked". Reception clears a morning of follow-ups in seconds instead of clicking through individually.

sections.heading: Per-member queue & stats
sections.body: Each team member's queue shows their open, overdue, snoozed and completed counts at a glance. A stats endpoint exposes follow-up performance for the operator who is investing in the program.

salon.title: Salons & Spas
salon.tagline: Color-coded staff lanes, memberships, loyalty and retail — tuned to the rhythm of a salon floor.
salon.long: Salons live on rebooks, retail attach-rate and stylist commissions. Momentfy's salon configuration ships with the Calendar module set to color-coded staff lanes and resource booking for chairs and rooms, the Catalogue with services, packages and tiered memberships, the Loyalty Points add-on for portal-redeemable rewards, Sales & POS with tip-split and per-stylist commission, and the branded Client Portal so guests rebook from your own URL — not a marketplace.
pains: Double-bookings and no-shows quietly burning 15–20% of the day's revenue
Memberships and prepaid packages tracked in a spreadsheet that is always one stylist behind
Commissions, tips and product splits recalculated by hand every payday
Loyalty punch-cards walking out the door with the client
capabilities: Drag-and-drop staff lanes with chair/room resource booking
Memberships, gift cards and package credits in one client wallet
Loyalty points auto-earned at POS, redeemable in the branded portal
Per-stylist commissions with tip-split and product attach
Retail inventory with re-order points, transfers and stocktakes
SMS / WhatsApp reminders and review-request campaigns
Walk-in queue rendered alongside booked appointments
End-of-day cash close with shift reconciliation and float drop
salon.whoFor: Hair, beauty, nails, lash and spa operators running 1–50 chairs across one or many locations.
useCases: Solo stylist taking online bookings with deposit-protected slots
Multi-chair salon with stylist-specific commissions and tip-split
Spa group selling seasonal memberships across branches
Retail-heavy concept bar upselling product attach at POS checkout

faq.q: Can stylists only see their own appointments?
faq.a: Yes — role-based scopes restrict each member to their own lane by default; managers see the full floor and can rebook across stylists.

faq.q: Do memberships auto-renew?
faq.a: Yes. Stripe or Lemon Squeezy charges the card on file and tops up the wallet credit automatically; clients can pause, swap tier or cancel from the portal.

faq.q: Can I split tips between stylist, washer and reception?
faq.a: Yes — tip-split rules are defined per service or per ticket and feed straight into the Team & HR pay run on commission night.

dental.title: Dental Clinics
dental.tagline: Charts, treatment plans, insurance and recall — built for the chair and the front desk.
dental.long: Dental clinics juggle clinical accuracy with patient flow. Momentfy ships with the Dental Chart add-on for pediatric and adult odontograms, the Insurance Classes add-on for coverage rules and claim-ready invoices, the Medical Records add-on for SOAP notes and attachments, the Calendar with chair resource booking and room blocks, and the Client Portal where patients confirm appointments, download prescriptions and pay balances — all stitched into the same client file.
pains: Paper charts and standalone imaging that never reach the front desk
Insurance claims rejected because coverage rules were applied at checkout, not at booking
Recall lists tracked manually — scaling and check-up patients never come back
Treatment plans signed off but never converted into actual sales
capabilities: Pediatric + adult odontogram with surface-level treatment status
Multi-stage treatment plans with phased estimates and consent capture
Insurance class rules applied at booking, not at checkout
Recall queue with auto-scheduled hygiene and check-up tasks
Lab orders and results attached to the patient timeline
Prescriptions authored in-app, downloadable from the portal
Chair / room resource booking with hygienist + dentist pairing
ZATCA / ETA-compliant invoicing on every visit
dental.whoFor: Solo practitioners, group practices and dental chains running 1–30 chairs with insurance mix.
useCases: Solo dentist tracking treatment phases per patient with photo attachments
Group practice with hygienists, GPs and specialists sharing one chair grid
Insurance-heavy clinic with two payers and coverage caps per service
Multi-branch chain consolidating revenue and insurance recovery in real time

faq.q: Is the chart pediatric-aware?
faq.a: Yes. The Dental Chart add-on toggles between deciduous and permanent dentition and lets you record surface-level findings, planned treatment and completed work side-by-side.

faq.q: How does insurance pricing work at the chair?
faq.a: Each service can be linked to one or more Insurance Classes with co-pay, ceiling and exclusion rules. The system splits the invoice into patient-due and insurer-due before checkout — no manual recalculation.

faq.q: Can I attach X-rays or photos to the patient file?
faq.a: Yes. The Clinical Imaging add-on stores DICOM and standard images against the patient timeline; the Client Portal can expose the ones you choose to share.

medical.title: Medical Clinics
medical.tagline: EMR, lab orders, prescriptions and a calm waiting room — for clinics that bill in days, not weeks.
medical.long: Medical clinics need clinical depth without enterprise weight. Momentfy combines the Medical Records add-on (structured SOAP notes, vitals and attachments), the Lab Orders add-on (order in, results back, published to portal), the Prescriptions add-on with patient-facing download, the Calendar with doctor + room resource booking, and the Client Portal for self-check-in and balance payment — all on the same patient file the front desk uses for invoicing and recall.
pains: Lab results and prescriptions arriving by email and getting lost
Charting that takes longer than the appointment itself
Insurance balances that age out before anyone notices
No simple way for patients to pre-fill intake before walking in
capabilities: Structured EMR with SOAP notes, vitals and attachments
Lab orders, results capture and patient-portal publishing
Prescriptions authored in-app with portal download
Doctor + room resource booking with reusable templates
Insurance class rules and claim-ready invoices
Patient self-check-in via QR or portal link
Recall and follow-up tasks with SLA tracking
AR aging by patient, payer and aging bucket
medical.whoFor: GP and specialty clinics running 1–20 doctors, with cash, insurance or hybrid billing.
useCases: GP clinic with shared rooms and rotating doctor schedules
Specialty practice ordering external labs and publishing results to patients
Polyclinic with insurance + cash billing on the same invoice
Tele-consultation flow with portal intake and digital prescriptions

faq.q: Does Momentfy replace a full EMR?
faq.a: For most outpatient clinics, yes — SOAP notes, vitals, history, lab orders and prescriptions live in one place. For deep specialty workflows we expose REST and webhooks so you can plug in a vertical EMR if needed.

faq.q: Can patients see only their own results?
faq.a: Yes. The Client Portal authenticates the patient and shows only what their record contains; you choose per-result whether it auto-publishes or stays internal.

faq.q: How are insurance balances tracked?
faq.a: Each invoice is split into patient-due and payer-due lines, both visible in AR aging. The Reports module tracks recovery rate and average days-to-pay per insurer.

barber.title: Barber Shops
barber.tagline: Walk-ins, queue boards, fade-card memberships and clean barber commissions.
barber.long: Barber shops live on walk-ins and regulars. Momentfy renders the Calendar as a shared queue board so the front desk can drop a walk-in into the next open chair, runs Memberships for unlimited-cut subscriptions, applies per-barber commissions through Team & HR, and powers a branded Client Portal where regulars rebook the same barber in two taps. The Loyalty add-on turns every visit into a rewarded touch.
pains: Walk-ins waiting in the doorway with no visible queue order
Members with prepaid packages who nobody can verify at the chair
Tip and commission math redone in a notebook every Sunday
Regulars who switch shops the moment their barber leaves
capabilities: Live queue board with walk-in slot insertion
Unlimited-cut memberships with per-barber caps
Loyalty points auto-earned per visit, redeemable at the chair
Per-barber commissions with tip-split and retail attach
SMS reminders and "your barber is free" alerts
Booth-rental mode with separate chair P&L
Cash close per barber and per shift
Client portal with one-tap rebook to the same barber
barber.whoFor: Single-shop barbers, multi-barber salons and booth-rental concepts running 1–20 chairs.
useCases: High-traffic shop running 80% walk-ins on a queue board
Booth-rental concept with each barber as their own micro-business
Membership-heavy shop selling 4-cut and unlimited monthly plans
Multi-location chain consolidating commission across branches

faq.q: How do walk-ins fit into a booked calendar?
faq.a: The queue board lives next to the booked grid; the front desk can drop a walk-in into the next open chair and the system shifts the visual order without moving real bookings.

faq.q: Can each barber see only their own queue?
faq.a: Yes — role-based scopes plus a "my chair" view show only that barber's queue and tickets, while managers keep the full floor view.

faq.q: Does Momentfy handle booth-rental P&L?
faq.a: Yes. Each chair can be configured as a sub-tenant with its own catalogue, prices and commission split — useful for shops where barbers are independent operators.

fitness.title: Fitness & Studios
fitness.tagline: Class scheduling, capacity, waitlists and member check-in — without the bloat of a gym CRM.
fitness.long: Fitness studios juggle group classes, 1-on-1 sessions, memberships and per-program tracking. Momentfy ships with the Events & Classes add-on for capacity and waitlists, the Client Attendance add-on for fast check-in, the Memberships catalogue with tiered access, the Calendar with trainer + room booking, and the Client Portal where members reserve, cancel within window and see their program progress side-by-side.
pains: Classes running at half-capacity while a waitlist sits unbooked
Members on legacy plans nobody can audit
Trainer commissions tied to attendance that nobody tallies
Last-minute cancels that never trigger the next person on the list
capabilities: Group classes with capacity, waitlist and auto-promotion
Recurring schedules with one-off overrides and instructor swaps
Membership tiers with class quotas and burn-down tracking
QR / NFC check-in with kiosk mode for the front desk
Trainer commissions per class, per member and per package
Cancellation windows with auto-credit policy enforcement
Client portal reservations with calendar-synced reminders
Reports on fill rate, churn risk and program completion
fitness.whoFor: Boutique studios, CrossFit boxes, yoga and pilates centers, fight gyms and personal-training operators.
useCases: Yoga studio with 40 classes a week and a single instructor team
Boxing gym selling 8-class packs alongside unlimited memberships
Pilates studio with reformer-equipment booking
Multi-branch chain with city-wide class hopping

faq.q: How does the waitlist actually promote people?
faq.a: When a slot opens (cancel inside or outside window) the next-in-line is auto-promoted and notified by SMS / push. They have a configurable hold period to confirm before the slot drops to the following member.

faq.q: Can I sell multi-tier memberships with class quotas?
faq.a: Yes. The Catalogue defines quotas per cycle (e.g. 8 classes / month, unlimited yoga), and the system burns them down at check-in. Top-ups, rollover and pause are all configurable per tier.

faq.q: Does check-in work without staff at the door?
faq.a: Yes. Kiosk mode with QR scanning lets members self-check-in; the system blocks expired members and members without a reservation, and prints a waiver if you require one.

pet.title: Vet & Pet Care
pet.tagline: Vaccines, boarding, grooming and reminders — one chart per pet, one wallet per owner.
pet.long: Vet clinics and pet groomers track two profiles at once: the pet (vaccines, weight, allergies) and the owner (wallet, communication preferences, payment terms). Momentfy ships with the Medical Records add-on tuned for veterinary fields, the Lab Orders and Prescriptions add-ons, the Calendar with kennel / grooming-station resource booking, recurring vaccine recalls via Follow-up Tasks, and the Client Portal where owners book grooming and confirm boarding pickup.
pains: Vaccine recalls slipping a year because nobody schedules the next one
Grooming rebooks left to "we'll call you" rather than booked at checkout
Boarding revenue lost to over- or under-booked kennels
Multi-pet households with mixed wallets and split invoices
capabilities: Pet profiles with breed, weight history, allergies and vaccine schedule
Vaccine and check-up recalls auto-scheduled by Follow-up Tasks
Boarding inventory with kennel-by-kennel availability
Grooming station resource booking with turn-time reminders
Owner wallet covering multiple pets on one invoice
Lab orders and prescriptions linked to the pet timeline
Loyalty points across grooming, retail and food
Portal photos and discharge notes after every visit
pet.whoFor: Vet clinics, mixed groomer-vet practices, pet boarding hotels and pet-supply retailers.
useCases: GP vet clinic running surgeries + walk-in consultations
Mobile groomer with route-based booking
Boarding hotel selling stays plus optional grooming
Multi-pet owner with one wallet covering 3 cats and a dog

faq.q: Can one owner have multiple pets on the same invoice?
faq.a: Yes. The owner is the billable entity; pets are linked records. Invoices can mix services across all of an owner's pets and the wallet stays on the owner.

faq.q: How do vaccine recalls actually fire?
faq.a: Each vaccine type carries a default cycle. When you record a dose, Follow-up Tasks schedules the next reminder by SMS / WhatsApp at the right interval; the front desk can override per-pet.

faq.q: Can the portal show vaccine history?
faq.a: Yes — owners log into the portal and see vaccine history, due dates, prescriptions and a download-ready summary for travel or boarding.

therapy.title: Therapy & Coaching
therapy.tagline: Privacy-first sessions, recurring blocks, encrypted notes — for therapists and coaches.
therapy.long: Therapy and coaching practices need a calendar that respects privacy and a notebook that respects clinical depth. Momentfy ships with the Calendar configured for recurring weekly blocks, encrypted SOAP notes via Medical Records, branded Client Portal intake forms with consent capture, the Catalogue for session packs and sliding-scale pricing, and Follow-up Tasks for between-session homework — all under role-based access so admins never see notes.
pains: Notes scattered across paper, Notion and email
Repeating weekly slots that break the moment a client moves a session
Cancellations inside policy with no automated charge
Intake forms collected on paper and re-keyed by hand
capabilities: Recurring weekly slots with smart move + cascade options
Encrypted session notes with SOAP and free-form templates
Session packs and sliding-scale pricing per client
Late-cancel fees auto-charged by policy window
Portal intake with digital consent and signed waivers
Between-session homework via Follow-up Tasks
Role-based access — admins never see clinical notes
Recurring invoicing for retainer-based engagements
therapy.whoFor: Solo therapists, group practices, coaches and counselors running by-appointment work.
useCases: Solo therapist with weekly recurring clients on sliding-scale fees
Group practice sharing rooms across multiple clinicians
Coach selling 6-session packs and quarterly retainers
Online-only counselor running portal-only intake and billing

faq.q: Are session notes private from front-desk staff?
faq.a: Yes — clinical notes are role-gated. By default only the assigned clinician (and explicitly granted supervisors) can read them; admin staff see appointment metadata and billing only.

faq.q: How does sliding-scale pricing work?
faq.a: Each client can carry a price override per service. The catalogue stores the standard rate; the override is applied at billing and shown on the portal so there's no surprise.

faq.q: Can I run a weekly recurring slot for years?
faq.a: Yes. Recurring rules generate the calendar lazily; if a client moves one session you choose whether it's a one-off shift or a permanent move-forward of the recurrence.

photo.title: Photo Studios
photo.tagline: Sessions, deposits, gallery delivery and balance-on-delivery — without losing the upsell.
photo.long: Photo studios live on deposit discipline and post-shoot upsells. Momentfy ships with the Calendar tuned for shoot blocks and golden-hour windows, the Catalogue with package + add-on pricing, deposit-protected booking, gallery delivery via the Client Portal, and POS-driven upsells (prints, frames, retouching) on balance day. Resources covers studios, props and rented gear so nothing double-books.
pains: Shoots booked without deposits — gone in a phone-call cancel
Final balance chased for weeks after delivery
Add-ons (extra retouch, prints) discussed but never charged
Gear and studio bookings clashing with no visible calendar
capabilities: Deposit-protected booking with auto-refund rules
Shoot packages with photo count, retouch hours and delivery SLA
Resource booking for studios, props and rented gear
Gallery delivery and print-store via the Client Portal
Balance-on-delivery POS flow with same-day card capture
Add-on upsell prompts (extra prints, frames, retouch tiers)
Per-photographer commission and assistant splits
Client review and referral campaigns post-delivery
photo.whoFor: Wedding, family, brand and editorial photographers running solo or as a small studio.
useCases: Wedding photographer collecting 30% deposit and balance-on-delivery
Family studio booking 30-minute mini-shoots back-to-back
Brand-content studio renting props + studio time as a package
Editorial team sharing rented gear across two crews

faq.q: Can deposits be partially refundable by date?
faq.a: Yes. Each package can carry a refund schedule (e.g. 100% > 14 days, 50% < 14 days, 0% < 48 hours). The system enforces it at cancellation and explains the rule on the portal.

faq.q: How is the gallery delivered?
faq.a: Galleries live behind the Client Portal — the client logs in, picks favourites, downloads originals and orders prints. You can gate downloads behind balance payment.

faq.q: Can I track gear that I rent for shoots?
faq.a: Yes. The Resources add-on covers cameras, lenses and studio space; each can be booked alongside a shoot and shown as unavailable while the shoot is live.

nails.title: Nail & Lash Bars
nails.tagline: Tight slot grids, deposit-on-book, retail attach and one-tap rebook.
nails.long: Nail and lash bars live on tight slot grids, deposit discipline and retail attach. Momentfy ships with the Calendar in 15-minute lanes, deposit-protected booking, the Loyalty Points add-on for repeat-visit rewards, retail Inventory for polish and lash trays, and the Client Portal where guests rebook the same artist in two taps and pre-pay the deposit on the same screen.
pains: Free-with-no-deposit bookings that vanish at noon
Polish stock running out mid-set with no re-order trigger
Loyalty cards forgotten in pockets, never punched
Rebook conversation skipped at checkout because the queue is loud
capabilities: 15-minute slot lanes with artist-specific service durations
Deposit-protected booking with cancellation-window rules
Loyalty points across nail, lash and retail
Retail inventory for polish, lash trays and aftercare
Per-artist commissions with tip-split
Pre-visit consult forms via the Client Portal
One-tap rebook to the same artist + service combo
SMS / WhatsApp aftercare and review-request flow
nails.whoFor: Nail salons, lash bars, brow studios and combined beauty concept bars.
useCases: High-volume nail salon with 6 chairs and 15-min slot lanes
Lash studio selling refill packages alongside one-off sets
Combined nail + lash bar sharing artists across services
Boutique brow studio with consult-then-treatment flow

faq.q: How strict is the deposit policy enforcement?
faq.a: You set the rule per service — fixed amount or percentage, refundable inside or outside a window. Online bookings cannot complete without payment if the rule says so.

faq.q: Can each artist have different service durations?
faq.a: Yes. The Catalogue stores a default duration and Team & HR overrides per member, so a senior artist's gel-set is 45 mins and a junior's is 60 mins on the same booking page.

faq.q: Do refill clients see only refill slots?
faq.a: Yes — service rules can require a prior set within a time window. Returning clients see refill availability; new clients are routed to a full-set slot.

events.title: Events Creation & Management
events.tagline: Tickets, capacity, waitlists and check-in — for one-off events and recurring programs.
events.long: Event organizers swing between one-night sell-throughs and recurring multi-week programs. Momentfy uses the Events & Classes add-on for ticket tiers, capacity and waitlists, the Client Attendance add-on for QR-based check-in, the Calendar for venue + room blocks, the Catalogue for early-bird and bundle pricing, and the Client Portal for self-serve registration and ticket download — all on the same client database the rest of the business runs on.
pains: Tickets sold on three platforms that never reconcile
Day-of check-in done with a printed list and a highlighter
Waitlists kept in a notes app that nobody re-reads
Refund and resale rules invented per email at 11pm
capabilities: Ticket tiers with early-bird, bundle and code-only access
Capacity caps with auto-promoting waitlist
QR check-in with door-list mode for staff phones
Recurring multi-week programs with per-week capacity
Refund and resale policy enforced by ticket tier
Bulk SMS / email blasts to ticket-holders
Vendor and venue resource booking alongside the event
Reports on sell-through, churn and ticket-source mix
events.whoFor: Event organizers, workshop producers, conference teams and recurring program operators.
useCases: One-night event with three ticket tiers and a VIP code
6-week workshop program with per-session capacity
Conference with sponsor-vendor booth booking
Multi-city tour with separate ticketing per stop

faq.q: Can I run early-bird and code-only tiers together?
faq.a: Yes. Each tier has its own price, capacity, valid window and access rule (open / code / member-only). The portal automatically shows or hides tiers per visitor context.

faq.q: How does day-of check-in work?
faq.a: Door staff scan ticket QRs from a phone. The system marks the ticket attended, prevents re-use, and feeds attendance back to the event report in real time.

faq.q: What happens to the waitlist when capacity opens?
faq.a: When a ticket is refunded or released, the next-in-line gets a hold link by SMS / email and a configurable window to claim the seat before it falls to the next person.

wellness.title: Massage & Wellness
wellness.tagline: Treatment rooms, packages, programs and aftercare — paced for a calm room.
wellness.long: Massage and wellness centers run programs, not appointments. Momentfy ships with the Calendar tuned for room + therapist resource booking, the Catalogue for multi-session packages and program journeys, the Memberships system for unlimited or capped plans, Follow-up Tasks for between-visit homework, and the Client Portal for intake forms, session history and at-home program steps — so guests come back not because of a reminder, but because they're mid-program.
pains: Programs sold once, never tracked, never resold
Therapist-room mismatch leading to a free room with a busy therapist
Aftercare advice lost the moment the guest leaves
Memberships paused informally and never resumed
capabilities: Program packages with sequenced sessions and progress tracking
Therapist + room resource booking with conflict prevention
Memberships with pause / resume and quota burn-down
Intake forms and consent waivers via the Client Portal
Aftercare and at-home steps via Follow-up Tasks
Couples and group bookings on linked rooms
Loyalty points across treatments, retail and product
Reports on program completion and resell rate
wellness.whoFor: Massage clinics, day spas, wellness retreats and program-driven recovery centers.
useCases: Day spa with 8 rooms and shared therapist pool
Recovery center selling 12-session programs with progress markers
Couples retreat booking paired rooms back-to-back
Wellness chain with city-wide program portability

faq.q: Can a guest pause a membership?
faq.a: Yes. Members can pause from the portal within policy (e.g. up to 30 days a year, no pause inside the first month); the system stops billing and stops quota burn until they resume.

faq.q: How are programs different from packages?
faq.a: Packages are buckets of credits; programs are sequenced — session 4 unlocks after sessions 1-3 are completed, with optional aftercare between each. Both live in the Catalogue.

faq.q: Can I link rooms for couples bookings?
faq.a: Yes — link two rooms in the Resources add-on and the booking flow blocks both at once, with one therapist per room or a shared pair.

driving.title: Driving Schools
driving.tagline: Vehicles, instructors, lesson packs and license-stage tracking.
driving.long: Driving schools schedule three resources at once — student, instructor and vehicle — and bill against multi-stage license programs. Momentfy ships with the Calendar configured for triple-resource booking via the Resources add-on, the Catalogue for staged lesson packs, the Client Portal for parent visibility and lesson confirmation, and Reports for vehicle utilization and pass-rate by instructor — so the next slot books itself.
pains: Vehicles double-booked across instructors
Students paying per-lesson with no view of how far they are in the program
Test-prep slots filled by students who haven't completed the prerequisites
Instructor commissions tied to hours that nobody logs accurately
capabilities: Triple-resource booking — student, instructor and vehicle
Staged license programs with prerequisite enforcement
Lesson packs with credit burn-down and rollover rules
Parent / guardian portal access for minor students
Vehicle utilization and maintenance reminders
Per-instructor commissions with hour and outcome bonuses
Pre-test readiness check before booking the test slot
Pass-rate and retest reports by instructor and vehicle
driving.whoFor: Driving schools, defensive-driving programs, motorcycle and commercial-license trainers.
useCases: License school with 12 vehicles and 30 instructors
Refresher-only school selling 3-lesson packs
Commercial truck-license program with stage-gated lessons
Multi-branch group with city-wide instructor mobility

faq.q: How do I prevent students from booking the test before they're ready?
faq.a: Each test slot can require completion of named prerequisites (e.g. 12 lessons, 1 highway session, 1 night session). The portal hides the slot until they're met.

faq.q: Can a vehicle be assigned a maintenance day?
faq.a: Yes. Vehicles are resources with their own blocked-time rules; you can schedule maintenance windows that stop bookings system-wide.

faq.q: Can parents see their child's progress?
faq.a: Yes. The portal supports a guardian role with read access to scheduled lessons, attended count and remaining package credits.

education.title: Education & Training
education.tagline: Cohorts, 1-on-1 sessions, term packs, attendance and a parent-role portal — for tutoring centers, language schools, training academies and exam-prep operators.
education.long: Education and training providers run cohorts, private sessions and recurring programs under one roof. Momentfy ships with the Events & Classes add-on for cohort capacity and waitlists, the Calendar for instructor + room booking, the Client Attendance add-on for sign-in, Follow-up Tasks for homework and reading lists, the Client Portal with a parent-role view, and Catalogue-driven term packs with rollover and sibling-discount tiers — so the front desk runs term enrolment from the same screen the teacher takes attendance on.
pains: Cohorts running below capacity while a waitlist exists for the next term
Homework given verbally and forgotten by Wednesday
Parents asking for last week's attendance via SMS at 9pm
Sibling discounts applied inconsistently between months
capabilities: Cohort + 1-on-1 scheduling with tutor and room booking
Term packs with rollover, sibling and pre-pay discounts
Homework and reading via Follow-up Tasks tied to the student
Parent portal with attendance, progress and balance
Per-tutor commissions on hours and outcomes
Auto check-in via QR with parent notification
Mock-test scoring and progress reports
SMS / WhatsApp nudges for missed homework
education.whoFor: After-school tutoring centers, language schools, music academies and exam-prep operators.
useCases: After-school center running 5-student cohorts plus 1-on-1s
Language school selling term packs with sibling discount
Music academy booking instrument-specific rooms
Exam-prep program with mock-test progress tracking

faq.q: Do parents get their own login?
faq.a: Yes. The Client Portal supports a parent role linked to one or more student records; parents see attendance, balances, homework and can reschedule within policy.

faq.q: Can I run cohorts and 1-on-1 lessons in the same calendar?
faq.a: Yes. The Calendar shows cohort blocks and 1-on-1 slots side-by-side; tutors can be assigned to both and the system prevents overlapping commitments.

faq.q: How do sibling discounts apply?
faq.a: Discount rules in the Catalogue can be tied to "household" — when two students share a parent record, the discount auto-applies to the second and subsequent enrollments.

professional.title: Professional Services
professional.tagline: Estimates, retainers, time entries and AR — without buying a separate billing tool.
professional.long: Professional services firms — agencies, consultants, accountants, law practices — bill against estimates, retainers and time. Momentfy ships with the Sales & POS module configured for estimates → invoices, recurring retainers via Memberships, time entries against client engagements, the Calendar for client + member meetings, and Reports for AR aging and billable capture by member — all without buying a separate billing tool on top.
pains: Estimates approved by email and never invoiced
Retainer clients tracked in a spreadsheet that drifts every month
Billable hours forgotten by the time they need to be billed
AR aging only visible at month-end when it's already too late
capabilities: Estimates with version history and one-click conversion to invoice
Recurring retainers with credit burn-down and rollover
Time entries against engagements, billable + non-billable
Engagement-level P&L by client and by member
AR aging by client and aging bucket with reminders
Branded client portal with quote / invoice / payment
Per-member commissions on billable revenue
Audit log for estimate / invoice changes
professional.whoFor: Agencies, consultants, accountants, law practices and B2B service teams running 2–50 members.
useCases: Agency on retainers + project work, billing monthly
Consulting firm tracking hours per engagement
Accounting practice with recurring monthly clients
Law office running matter-based time entry and billing

faq.q: Can estimates auto-become invoices?
faq.a: Yes. Once a client approves the estimate (digitally, from the portal) it converts to an invoice with the same line items; you can configure auto-send or hold for review.

faq.q: How do retainers handle rollover?
faq.a: Each retainer plan stores a credit pool and a rollover policy (none / capped / unlimited). Burn-down happens at time-entry approval; the portal shows the client their remaining credit.

faq.q: Does Momentfy track non-billable time too?
faq.a: Yes. Members log time as billable or non-billable against an engagement. The Reports module shows utilization and effective rate per member.

auto.title: Auto Service & Maintenance
auto.tagline: Bays, parts, multi-stage jobs and service recall — for shops that bill on the same day.
auto.long: Auto-service shops bill against vehicles, parts and multi-stage jobs. Momentfy ships with the Calendar configured for bay + technician resource booking via Resources, multi-stage job tickets in Sales & POS, parts-and-labor invoicing with stock movements through Inventory, vehicle profiles in the client record, and Follow-up Tasks for next-service recall — so a returning customer is greeted by name and by VIN.
pains: Parts billed on a paper ticket and forgotten on the invoice
Bays sitting empty between drop-off and write-up
Service intervals tracked in the customer's head, not yours
Insurance claims and warranty work treated like cash jobs
capabilities: Multi-stage job tickets (drop-off, diagnosis, repair, QC, pickup)
Bay + technician resource booking via the Resources add-on
Parts inventory with VIN-linked usage and supplier reorder
Vehicle profiles per customer with service history
Service-interval recalls via Follow-up Tasks
Insurance / warranty job flow with payer-vs-customer split
Per-tech commission on labor and per-service add-on
Reports on bay utilization, parts margin and rebook rate
auto.whoFor: Independent garages, multi-bay shops, tire centers and specialty service chains.
useCases: 4-bay shop with mechanical + bodywork
Tire / wheel chain with seasonal swap-over recalls
Specialist EV / hybrid shop with parts pre-ordering
Multi-branch group consolidating parts and labor margins

faq.q: How are vehicles linked to a customer?
faq.a: Each customer can carry multiple vehicles (VIN, plate, year, mileage). Job tickets are scoped to a vehicle so service history and recall rules attach to the right car, not just the owner.

faq.q: Can I run an insurance job and a cash job on the same invoice?
faq.a: Yes. Lines can be split between payer (insurer / warranty / customer); the invoice shows totals per payer and AR aging tracks them separately.

faq.q: Do bays show occupancy in real time?
faq.a: Yes. The Resources add-on renders bays as bookable resources; the calendar shows who's in which bay, what stage they're at, and what's queued next.

optical.title: Optical & Eyewear
optical.tagline: Eye exams, frames, prescriptions and renewals — for shops that sell time and stock.
optical.long: Optical shops blend a clinical workflow (exam, prescription) with a retail one (frames, lenses, contacts). Momentfy ships with the Calendar for optometrist booking, the Medical Records add-on for exam history, the Prescriptions add-on with portal download, the Catalogue + Inventory pairing for frames and lens combinations, and Follow-up Tasks for annual exam recalls — so frame attach happens at the chair, not later.
pains: Frame stockouts in the colour the customer just tried on
Annual exam reminders happening 3 months late
Prescriptions printed on paper and re-keyed at the lens lab
Frame-only sales with no optometrist time captured
capabilities: Exam booking with optometrist + room resources
Prescription history with portal-side download
Frame inventory with colour / size variants
Lens-build catalogue (single-vision, progressive, coatings)
Annual exam recalls via Follow-up Tasks
Insurance vision-care class rules at booking

Reports on attach rate, renewal and stock turnover
optical.whoFor: Independent opticians, optical chains and combined optometry + retail clinics.
useCases: Single-shop optometrist with retail frame wall
Optical chain consolidating frame stock across branches
Vision-insurance heavy shop with payer rules at booking
Subscription contact-lens program with auto-renewal

faq.q: Can I link a prescription to a sale of frames + lenses?
faq.a: Yes. The prescription is stored on the patient timeline; you can attach it to a sale, ship it to a lens lab partner, and update the order status from a single ticket.

faq.q: How do annual recalls actually fire?
faq.a: Follow-up Tasks sends an SMS / email a configurable number of months after the last exam. Customers can rebook directly from the link, and the front desk sees overdue customers in a queue.

faq.q: Do you handle frame variants?
faq.a: Yes. Frames can be defined with colour and size variants; each variant has its own SKU, stock count and re-order point so the wall always reflects what's actually buyable.

carwash.title: Car Wash & Detailing
carwash.tagline: Memberships, lane queues, detail upsell and water-saving reports.
carwash.long: Car wash and detailing operators live on memberships and same-day upsells. Momentfy ships with the Calendar tuned for lane queues, the Memberships system for unlimited-wash plans with vehicle-binding, the Catalogue with detail-tier upsells, the Client Portal where members manage their plan and rebook detail, and Sales & POS for lane-side checkout — with Reports tracking member MRR and detail attach.

Lanes idle while a queue formed at the entrance
Auto-renew failures hidden until the next visit

Multi-bay detail shop offering 4-tier packages
Multi-branch subscription brand with city-wide access

faq.q: How do plate-bound memberships prevent sharing?
faq.a: Each membership is bound to one or more declared license plates. Visits without a registered plate prompt a confirmation step; second-vehicle add-ons are charged at the configured price.

faq.q: Can I run lane queues without staff phones?
faq.a: Yes. A wall-mounted board can show lane status; staff at the lane use a tablet with a lane-side checkout view that closes the ticket and prints / texts a receipt.

faq.q: What happens when auto-renew fails?
faq.a: The system retries on a configurable schedule, notifies the member by SMS / email, and shows the failed status in the lane app so the front desk can collect at the next visit.

coworking.title: Co-working Spaces
coworking.tagline: Memberships, meeting rooms, day passes and self-serve check-in.
coworking.long: Co-working operators sell time and space — desks, rooms and event slots. Momentfy ships with the Resources add-on for room and desk booking, the Memberships catalogue for monthly plans with credits, the Client Portal where members book rooms and pay for overage, the Client Attendance add-on for QR-based entry, and Reports for utilization, member churn and add-on revenue — running the whole space on the modules you already have.

Day passes sold informally and never billed properly
Member overage on rooms forgiven at month-end because the math is messy
No view of which members are quietly disengaging before they churn

Day-pass sales with QR entry via the Client Attendance add-on
Member portal with overage billing and balance top-ups
Event-space booking and after-hours rules
Auto-renew memberships with pause and tier-swap
Per-tenant white-label for franchise / managed locations
Mailroom / package log against member profiles
Reports on utilization, churn and add-on revenue

Shared studio space booking by the hour

faq.q: How is meeting-room time deducted from a membership?
faq.a: Each plan can include monthly room credits. Bookings burn down credits in real time; overage rolls onto a portal-side invoice with auto-charge or pay-on-checkout settings.

faq.q: Can guests buy a one-day pass without becoming members?
faq.a: Yes. Day passes are catalogue items with QR entry valid for the day; the Client Attendance add-on enforces single-use and start / end windows.

faq.q: Can I run a multi-city brand?
faq.a: Yes — the multi-tenant deployment lets each location have its own pricing and rooms while sharing the member directory; members move between cities without re-signing.

multi-tenant.title: Multi-location chains
multi-tenant.tagline: One codebase, many brands, branch-level reporting and central ops.
multi-tenant.long: Chains and franchise operators need central oversight without giving up local speed. Momentfy runs one deployment that serves unlimited tenants — each with its own branding, locale, pricing, staff and reports — rolled up into a central super-admin view with cross-tenant Reports, stock transfers between branches, and brand-level campaigns that branches opt into. Every module — Calendar, Sales, Inventory, HR, Accounting — works tenant-aware out of the box.
pains: Each branch on its own spreadsheet, with HQ chasing P&L by email
Consolidated reports a month late and reconciled by hand
Brand pricing drifting between locations without anyone noticing
No way to share stock across branches when one runs out

faq.q: Is each branch its own database or one shared one?
faq.a: One shared deployment with logical tenant isolation — every record carries a tenant scope. You get the operational speed of one system with the data isolation of separate ones.

faq.q: Can branches sell at different prices?
faq.a: Yes. The Catalogue stores brand-level prices; each branch can override on the items it wants and inherit the rest, so brand strategy and local pricing co-exist.

faq.q: How do I onboard a new branch?
faq.a: Provision a tenant from super-admin (under a day, typically), import or fork the catalogue, set local branding and pricing, invite staff. Reports start rolling up the moment the first sale lands.

mobile.title: Mobile & on-site teams
mobile.tagline: On-site routes, mobile invoices, instant payment — for teams that bill from the van.
mobile.long: Mobile and on-site teams bill at the kerb, not at a counter. Momentfy ships with the Calendar tuned for route-based scheduling, mobile-first Sales & POS for invoices on the spot, Stripe / Lemon Squeezy or cash collection at the door, the Client Portal for repeat bookings and invoice download, and Inventory + Resources tracking the parts and gear loaded onto each van — so the team that arrives knows the customer, the address and the last service.

Routes built in a notebook with no view of utilization

Customer asking for a receipt at the door with nothing to give them

faq.q: Does the mobile app work offline?
faq.a: Yes. Tickets, invoices and stock movements are captured offline and sync when the device is back online; the office sees them appear in real time after sync.

faq.q: How do I collect payment at the door?
faq.a: You can charge a stored card, send a one-tap payment link by SMS / WhatsApp, or take cash and reconcile at end-of-day. Stripe and Lemon Squeezy connectors handle card-on-file and tap-to-pay where available.

faq.q: Can I track which parts left the warehouse on which van?
faq.a: Yes. Inventory + Resources lets each vehicle act as a stock location; transfers in and out are logged, and the end-of-day reconciliation report flags shortfalls.

memberships.title: Membership businesses
memberships.tagline: Tiers, auto-renew, dunning, portal self-serve — for any business that bills on a cycle.
memberships.long: Membership businesses live on monthly recurring revenue, auto-renew success and churn discipline. Momentfy ships with a full Memberships system — tiers, quotas, auto-renew with smart retries, pause / swap / cancel from the Client Portal, dunning emails and SMS, gift and referral codes, plus the Reports module surfacing churn cohorts and upgrade opportunities. The Loyalty Points add-on layers retention rewards on top of the plan itself.

Members ghosting without a chance to pause and stay

faq.q: How does smart-retry handle failed renewals?
faq.a: Failed charges retry on a configurable schedule (e.g. 1d, 3d, 7d) with member SMS / email notifications between attempts; the front desk also sees a "needs attention" queue so they can collect on the next visit.

faq.q: Can members pause without losing their plan?
faq.a: Yes. Pause rules are tier-defined (allowed window, max days per year). The plan keeps its renewal date but billing and quota burn pause until the member resumes.

faq.q: Can I run gift and referral codes alongside tiers?
faq.a: Yes. Codes can grant a fixed discount, a credit-on-account, or a tier upgrade for a window. Referral attribution rolls into the inviter's loyalty wallet at activation.
