featuresPage.sub: Twenty-one major feature areas. Hundreds of screens. Every small detail wired to the next — all included in the source, all yours on day one.

items.title: Calendar & Scheduling
items.summary: The command center of any service business — every team member, every branch, every room or chair they touch, with reminders, payments and a public booking page wired in.

sections.heading: Calendar that fits how you work
sections.body: Day, three-day, week, month and agenda views, switchable in one tap. The day moves with a live "now" line, and a one-tap button snaps you back to the current hour when you've scrolled away. Phones default to the agenda so the team can run from a tablet at the door.

sections.heading: One column per staff member
sections.body: In day view, every staff member gets their own lane with their shifts and breaks visible — so you see who's free at a glance. Tap any empty slot and a new appointment opens pre-filled with that time and that member.

sections.heading: Spot busy days at a glance
sections.body: The date picker dots every day in the next three months that already has a booking — the front desk can quote an opening without scanning the whole month.

sections.heading: Stack services on one appointment
sections.body: A single appointment can chain multiple services across different staff — the second service starts where the first ends. Peak-hour pricing kicks in automatically and packages flow through to checkout as one bundle.

sections.heading: Walk-in or known client
sections.body: Pick a known client from a fast search by name, phone or email, or capture a walk-in with just a name and a phone — their details stay visible on the appointment.

sections.heading: No checkout, no start
sections.body: Each appointment moves through Booked → Confirmed → In progress → Completed, but it can't move to In progress until the client has paid. The front desk physically can't skip the till, so revenue never leaks.

sections.heading: Pay from the appointment
sections.body: Open the appointment, tap Checkout, and the till opens with the booked services pre-loaded. The receipt links back to the appointment, which now shows as Paid.

sections.heading: Reminders and auto no-show
sections.body: Clients get a 24-hour reminder over the channel they prefer — SMS, email, WhatsApp or push — on your brand. If they don't show up, the appointment auto-marks "no-show" after the grace period you set, so reports stay honest.

sections.heading: Block time for vacation, training, lunch
sections.body: Block any slot — daily, weekly or monthly until an end date — with colored reasons you manage in one place. Edit the whole recurring series at once when plans change.

sections.heading: Waitlist that closes the loop
sections.body: Queue clients for a service with their preferred dates and times. When a spot opens, notify the next person in line and book them straight from the waitlist — no hunting through a notebook.

sections.heading: Reserve the room or the chair
sections.body: Define rooms, dental chairs, studios or any equipment, link them to the services that need them, and the system reserves the right one inside every appointment so you never double-book gear.

sections.heading: Scan a ticket, jump to the booking
sections.body: Scan the appointment QR — from a printed slip, receipt or the client's phone — and the calendar jumps straight there. Notification links work the same way, so a tap on a missed-appointment alert lands you on the right slot.

sections.heading: Make the calendar match your business
sections.body: Color by service category or by appointment status, hide blocked time when you don't want it visible, choose Sunday or Monday as the week start, and decide whether to allow bookings outside hours, in the past or overlapping.

sections.heading: Public booking page, Google Calendar, Zoom
sections.body: Publish a public booking page where clients book and prepay through Stripe, Tap, Paymob, Tabby or Tamara — with your minimum notice, advance window and cancellation policy. Active add-ons sync every appointment to Google Calendar both ways and create a Zoom link automatically for online sessions.

sections.heading: Clinical shortcuts on the appointment
sections.body: When clinical add-ons are active, the appointment detail offers one-tap shortcuts to start a medical record, prescription, lab order or dental chart for the client — already pre-bound to that visit.

sections.heading: Multiple branches, each in their timezone
sections.body: Every appointment lives in a branch and every branch has its own timezone, so working hours, reminders and dates render correctly per location. Switch the active branch and the whole calendar follows.

items.title: Sales, POS & Invoicing
items.summary: From the daily till to the recurring invoice — every way money comes into the business, in one place, all flowing into your books automatically.

sections.heading: Lightning-fast point of sale
sections.body: Sell services, products, packages and memberships from one screen. Add a known client or ring up a walk-in, scan a barcode, hold a ticket while you serve someone else, and resume it later — your busiest day with zero friction.

sections.heading: Mix and match payments
sections.body: Split a single ticket across cash, card, bank transfer, online payment, Tabby and Tamara installments, store credit, gift cards, loyalty points and insurance — every method gets its own line on the receipt.

sections.heading: The day at a glance
sections.body: Open today's summary to see what you sold, what was refunded, what was collected and how much came in tips — broken down by item type and payment method, without exporting anything.

sections.heading: Searchable transaction history
sections.body: Every sale lives in a filterable list with a one-tap refund or void from the row. Larger refunds can require a second person to approve before any money moves, so nothing slips through.

sections.heading: Every payment in one stream
sections.body: A live feed of every payment ever taken, filterable by method, so "how much did we take in card today?" is a five-second answer, not a five-minute spreadsheet.

sections.heading: Invoices for work off the counter
sections.body: Send a polished invoice for jobs that didn't ring up at the till. See when the client opened it, took a partial payment, paid in full or fell overdue — and every invoice carries its own pay-online link the client can use from their phone.

sections.heading: Estimates that turn into invoices
sections.body: Quote work first, share a clean public link the client can review on their phone, and convert the accepted estimate to a paid invoice with one tap when they say yes.

sections.heading: Recurring billing on autopilot
sections.body: Schedule daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly or yearly invoices for retainers, memberships and subscriptions. Turn on auto-send and clients receive each one the moment it generates.

sections.heading: Automatic chasing of late payers
sections.body: Overdue invoices get polite reminders at 7, 21, 45 and 90 days on your behalf — and every reminder ships with a pay-online link the client can use without picking up the phone.

sections.heading: Gift cards from issue to redeem
sections.body: Issue a card with a recipient name and optional expiry; the system generates a unique code that's easy to read aloud. Clients redeem at the counter, your cashier checks the remaining balance with the same code.

sections.heading: Expenses with optional approval
sections.body: Log day-to-day costs against vendors and categories. Larger expenses can require approval before they post, and every approved expense lands in your books automatically — no double entry.

sections.heading: Credit notes that close the loop
sections.body: Issue store credit when you can't refund cash, apply it to a future invoice in one tap, and watch the balance count down as the client uses it.

sections.heading: Cash register, day in and day out
sections.body: Open the till with a starting balance, watch every cash-in and cash-out throughout the day, then close it to compare expected vs counted — the system tells you whether you're balanced, over or short, and keeps a full history of every session.

sections.heading: Receipts on paper or by link
sections.body: Print to a thermal receipt printer, share a public receipt link by SMS, or hand the client a pay-online link for any unpaid invoice — same flow whether the sale closed in-store or online. ZATCA and Egyptian e-invoicing numbers print on every document when those compliance add-ons are active.

sections.heading: Wired to your books from the first receipt
sections.body: Every sale, refund, gift-card issuance, credit note and approved expense lands in the right account in your books automatically — Reports and Accounting just roll up. You never type a journal entry to keep up with the till.

items.title: Clients & CRM
items.summary: Know every client. Nudge the right ones at the right moment. Keep the ones you've already earned — with one record, one consent register and a self-service portal.

sections.heading: Searchable client list with bulk import
sections.body: Search, filter, sort and export the whole list in seconds. Bring an existing client database in by uploading a CSV or Excel file from the template provided — saving hours of typing on day one.

sections.heading: Rich client records
sections.body: One screen captures everything you need: contact details, date of birth, allergies, free-form notes, your own tags, and — for B2B clients — the legal name, VAT and CR registration that ZATCA-compliant invoices require.

sections.heading: One client, every interaction
sections.body: Each client opens to a sidebar that includes appointments, sales, payments, invoices, notes, communications and notifications — plus medical, dental, lab, prescriptions, imaging, loyalty and insurance tabs when those add-ons are active.

sections.heading: Lifetime value at a glance
sections.body: The overview shows total spend, total visits, cancellations, no-shows and any open store credit — so the front desk knows whether to thank, follow up or apologize before the conversation starts.

sections.heading: Pinned notes the team can trust
sections.body: Any team member can add, edit and pin a note to the client. Pinned notes float to the top, every note carries the author and date, and the timeline survives across visits — institutional memory by default.

sections.heading: Communications timeline
sections.body: Every message ever sent to this client across in-app, email, SMS and WhatsApp — with subject, body, status and reason if it failed. "Did they actually get the reminder?" answered in two clicks.

sections.heading: Each client controls their channels
sections.body: Every client carries separate consent for appointment messages and marketing — toggleable per channel. Campaign blasts skip clients who haven't consented and count them in a dedicated "skipped" tally so you stay compliant without thinking about it.

sections.heading: Multiple addresses and emergency contacts
sections.body: Capture as many addresses (Home, Work, Site) and emergency contacts as a client needs — useful for clinics, mobile-service businesses and B2B accounts that have several billing locations.

sections.heading: Live segments — new, loyal, lapsed, big spenders
sections.body: Seven ready-made segments — New clients, Recent visitors, First-visit only, Loyal, Lapsed, High spenders and Upcoming birthdays — tunable on the spot with the count refreshing as you change the rules. Pin any segment as a colored badge that follows the client around the dashboard.

sections.heading: Send a campaign from any segment
sections.body: One tap on a segment opens the campaign builder pre-filled with its members. The export-upload-list cycle disappears — you can be sending an offer to lapsed clients in under a minute.

sections.heading: Email, SMS and WhatsApp campaigns
sections.body: Pick a segment, pick a channel, write the message and send. Watch the dashboard tick up sent, failed and skipped counts in real time, and the segment definition is locked in at send-time so the report is the same in six months.

sections.heading: Reviews with reply
sections.body: Every completed appointment can collect a 1–5 star review with a comment. Reply from the dashboard, hide a review with one tap if it needs context, and per-service averages flow onto the public booking page where they help close new bookings.

sections.heading: Tickets per client
sections.body: Each client has their own little support inbox. Open / closed status, message threads and a small blue dot when the last reply came from the client — so nothing slips through.

sections.heading: Client statement and store-credit balance
sections.body: Print or email a clean statement of charges and payments at any time. Open store credit from credit notes appears as its own line so the cashier can apply it on the next ticket.

sections.heading: Photo or built-in avatar
sections.body: Upload a client photo or pick from the avatar gallery; remove or change it any time. The avatar follows the client across the dashboard so the team always recognises who they're greeting.

sections.heading: Self-service portal and "log in as client"
sections.body: When you enable a portal account for a client, they can sign in to view bookings, invoices and memberships and change their own contact details. Admins can reset the password and "log in as client" for white-glove support — without ever asking the customer to share their password.

sections.heading: Loyalty wallet and follow-up tasks (add-ons)
sections.body: Add the Loyalty add-on and every client gets a points wallet with earn, redeem and expiry tracking. Add the Follow-up add-on and the profile gains a tasks tab with assignees, due dates and an automatic schedule for the touchpoints you don't want to forget.

items.title: Marketing & Campaigns
items.summary: Turn your client list into repeat revenue — campaigns that fire on the right channel, at the right moment, in your own brand's voice, and reminders the team never has to send by hand.

sections.heading: Campaigns to any client segment
sections.body: Pick a segment — new clients, lapsed clients, big spenders, anyone with a birthday next week — write the message and send. The system locks in the segment at send-time so the report you read in six months is the same one you read tomorrow.

sections.heading: Know the cost before you press send
sections.body: As you change segment or channel, the builder shows three live numbers: how many people are in the segment, how many will actually receive (after consent), and how many will be skipped — so there are no surprises on the bill.

sections.heading: Big sends, no spinning beach ball
sections.body: Small blasts go out instantly. Larger ones run in the background while the detail page updates the sent and failed counters every few seconds — so you can close your laptop without losing the thread.

sections.heading: See exactly who got it and who didn't
sections.body: Every recipient is on the delivery log with their status and the reason for any failure, in the campaign and again on the client's profile. "Why didn't Mrs. Khan get it?" is a one-look answer.

sections.heading: Reminders the team never sends
sections.body: Appointment reminders 24 hours before, an optional reminder one hour before, thank-yous after visits and tips, rebook reminders for clients who haven't been back, birthday greetings, win-back for the silent ones, and welcomes for new clients — all toggleable, all on your brand.

sections.heading: Operational alerts to the right person
sections.body: Low-stock alerts, membership expiry warnings, appointment cancellations, no-show alerts and an end-of-day summary — routed to owners, admins or specific roles only, so the team gets what they need and isn't drowned in pings.

sections.heading: Each transactional message follows your rules
sections.body: For every kind of message, you decide who hears about it (clients, staff, or both), on which channels, and which staff roles are included. One rule book governs every email, SMS, WhatsApp and in-app notification — so nothing goes out the door against policy.

sections.heading: Templates you can rewrite
sections.body: Every transactional message has its own template per channel, with placeholders like client name, service name, date and time the system fills in. Edit once and every future reminder uses your wording — no guessing about what variables exist.

sections.heading: Per-client consent that's respected automatically
sections.body: Each client says yes or no to appointment messages and to marketing — separately, per channel. Campaigns honor both, and clients who haven't consented are counted as "skipped" rather than "failed" so your delivery reports stay clean.

sections.heading: Each staff member mutes what they don't need
sections.body: Any team member can silence specific alerts on themselves without affecting their teammates — so the receptionist isn't pinged about pay-runs and the accountant isn't pinged about every booking.

sections.heading: Sent at the right local time, every time
sections.body: Birthday greetings go out at the time you choose. Rebook and win-back messages go out at a sensible local hour. A salon in Riyadh and a clinic in Cairo each get the timing right — no shared global clock.

sections.heading: Full audit log of everything sent
sections.body: Every outbound message is kept with the channel, recipient, subject, body, delivery status, gateway-side ID and any error text. Search by client, by event or by date — the answer is always one search away.

sections.heading: Bring your own gateways
sections.body: Send email through SMTP, SendGrid or Resend; send SMS through Twilio; send WhatsApp through Twilio or Meta. Credentials are stored encrypted, and a test-send button on each channel lets you verify the connection before a real client sees a single message.

items.title: Catalogue
items.summary: Your entire offering — services, products, packages, memberships, discounts and smart-pricing rules — set up once and priced with precision, ready to be sold from any branch.

sections.heading: Services with duration and price
sections.body: Set up each service with a name, description, color, icon, duration, price and tax. Add an optional processing time so back-to-back bookings don't trample on each other, and mark services as in-person, online or both — with a meeting link auto-attached for the online ones.

sections.heading: Categories that organise the menu
sections.body: Group services into categories with a color. The category color tints appointments on the calendar (when you choose that mode) and powers the per-category ratings shown on your public booking page.

sections.heading: Sell only where it's available
sections.body: Restrict a service, package, product or membership to specific branches — leave the list empty and it's available everywhere. So a single business can carry a Riyadh-only treatment, a lounge-only product, and a city-wide service all in the same catalogue.

sections.heading: Pair services with the rooms they need
sections.body: Define the rooms, chairs, dental units or studios you have, link them to the services that need them, and the calendar will reserve the right one inside every appointment — so equipment is never double-booked.

sections.heading: Per-staff commission on a service
sections.body: Override the default commission for a specific staff member on a specific service — useful when a senior therapist takes a higher cut on premium services. The system applies the right number on every ticket.

sections.heading: Bundles and packages
sections.body: Group several services into a bundle with a single price. Choose the pricing model (sum of line prices, a custom retail price, or a percentage off) and tell the calendar whether the services run one after another or together. Bundles can carry photos and a public-booking toggle of their own.

sections.heading: Memberships clients buy and use
sections.body: Sell memberships with a fixed duration and billing cycle, free-form benefits, an optional cap on appointments and an optional discount that applies to every service bought during the active period. Clients see their card, allowance and end date in their portal.

sections.heading: Memberships recognised the right way
sections.body: Selling a year-long membership doesn't show as a year of revenue overnight — earned portions move to revenue month by month over the membership window, the way IFRS and US GAAP expect. No spreadsheets, no manual entries.

sections.heading: Expiry messages and freeze
sections.body: Members get a heads-up before their membership expires and a notice when it does. Need to pause one for a vacation? Freeze it and the clock stops without ending the plan.

sections.heading: Retail products with brand and units
sections.body: Each product has a name, short and long description, brand, optional barcode, unit of measure (ml, L, g, kg, oz, fl-oz, pieces, units) and a flag for whether it's sold retail. Barcode and SKU are unique within your business.

sections.heading: Variants for size, color or batch
sections.body: Sell the same product in multiple sizes, colors or batches — each with its own SKU, barcode, price, cost, stock level and reorder threshold. Variants flow through sales, transfers, invoices and estimates as first-class items.

sections.heading: Commission on retail products too
sections.body: Switch on commissions for any product and set the percentage or flat amount. When a stylist or therapist sells it, their commission is calculated on the same ticket as their service work.

sections.heading: Discounts and promo codes
sections.body: Set up automatic discounts (apply on eligibility) or code-based discounts (the cashier types it or the client redeems it). Choose between a percentage off, a fixed amount off, or buy-X-get-Y. Apply to a single item, a category, or the whole cart.

sections.heading: Discount rules that match your business
sections.body: Limit a discount to specific products, services, categories or members; cap how many times it can be used in total or per client; set minimum purchase or minimum item count; and schedule it to fire only on, say, Mondays 6–10 PM. Multiple discounts stack in the order you choose, and you can lock one as exclusive.

sections.heading: Smart pricing for peak hours
sections.body: Create rules that automatically raise or lower prices for chosen services during specific day-and-time windows — Friday afternoon, Sunday morning, after 8 PM. Apply tenant-wide or to a single branch, and the new sale screen previews the adjusted price live.

sections.heading: ZATCA & ETA item codes ready to go
sections.body: Every service and product can carry the item codes Saudi (ZATCA) and Egyptian (ETA) e-invoicing require. Once entered, every compliance invoice ships with the right identifiers — no last-minute scramble at submission time.

items.title: Inventory & Supply
items.summary: Stock you can trust — every product in every branch, every receive and stocktake flowing into your books, with the audit trail to prove it.

sections.heading: Stock per branch, total at a glance
sections.body: Every branch has its own stock count and its own low-stock threshold per product, but the dashboard always shows the total across all branches — recomputed automatically after every sale, transfer or count. No one ever asks "is this number current?" again.

sections.heading: Low-stock alerts to the right person
sections.body: Every morning the system scans every product in every branch and pings the owner and inventory manager about anything that's hit its threshold — by in-app, email, SMS or WhatsApp, on the channels you've enabled. The rest of the team isn't bothered.

sections.heading: SKUs, barcodes, scan and print
sections.body: Every product gets an SKU and a barcode (generated for you with a tap if you don't have one), a printable label that sends to a thermal label printer, and a built-in scanner that opens the right product the instant you point the camera at a barcode.

sections.heading: Variants for size, color, batch
sections.body: Sell the same product in multiple sizes, colors or batches — each with its own SKU, barcode, price, cost, stock and reorder threshold. Variants flow through sales, transfers, invoices and estimates as first-class items.

sections.heading: Move stock between branches with an audit trail
sections.body: Create a transfer, the system checks that the source branch actually has the stock, send it, and the receiving branch confirms what arrived line by line — including damage notes for anything broken or short. Send, receive and cancel are separate actions so the audit trail stays clean.

sections.heading: Stocktakes with the variance you can act on
sections.body: Run a count, enter what you actually have, and the system shows the discrepancy line by line — search the list, filter to discrepancies only, and complete the count to overwrite the system stock with reality.

sections.heading: Purchase orders to suppliers
sections.body: Build a purchase order with a search-to-add product picker, per-line quantity and cost price, and live totals for cost and units. Track it from draft through ordered and received, with the payment status (unpaid, partially paid, paid) shown alongside.

sections.heading: Suppliers with map and history
sections.body: Keep each supplier's name, contact, email, phone, website, address (with a map and "get directions" link) and notes. The detail page shows the products you buy from them and a history of every order. Bulk-import suppliers from a template, deactivate or reactivate, and export the list any time.

sections.heading: Supplier payments and non-inventory bills
sections.body: Pay suppliers by cash, card, bank transfer or other method. A payment can settle a purchase order, a bill (for rent, utilities or marketing) or stand on its own. Outstanding balances roll up automatically and the payment links to the matching bank transaction for reconciliation.

sections.heading: Manual stock adjustment
sections.body: Need to write off a damaged unit or correct a count? Adjust a single product on the active branch with a mandatory reason. Reachable from the pencil on every stock row, from a barcode scan, or from the end of a stocktake.

sections.heading: All branches side by side
sections.body: One screen shows every product across every branch with a total column. Rows below a branch's threshold get a warning tint, and a single tap filters to low-stock only — perfect for deciding what to transfer where, on a phone or a screen.

sections.heading: Inventory report at a glance
sections.body: Open the report to see total active products, the twenty worst low-stock items (with their branch and current count), the top ten sellers in your date range, and the total stock valuation — exportable like every other report.

sections.heading: Receives and sales hit your books
sections.body: Receiving a purchase order books inventory against accounts payable. Paying the supplier draws AP down. Selling a product with a cost price books cost-of-goods-sold against inventory automatically — so margins, AP and stock valuation are always live in Reports and Accounting.

sections.heading: Bulk import, exports and audit log
sections.body: Bring products and suppliers in from CSV or Excel using the templates. Export any list (stock, transfers, counts, orders, suppliers, payments) with your current filters. Every change is captured in the activity log — the auditor sees exactly who did what and when.

items.title: Events & Ticketing
items.summary: Sell out your classes, workshops and events — tiered tickets, QR check-in, waitlists, online or in-person, with reminders and revenue all flowing into the same place. Activated as the Events add-on.

sections.heading: Set up an event in minutes
sections.body: Title, description, cover image, category, optional venue, optional organizer, start and end dates (multi-day works), times, and the event's own timezone — so a Riyadh business can publish a Cairo event without confusing anyone.

sections.heading: In-person, online or hybrid
sections.body: Choose the format and add a meeting link, ID and password for online events. With the Zoom add-on active, the meeting link is created for you and stays in sync with the event.

sections.heading: Ticket tiers (General, VIP, Early Bird)
sections.body: Add as many ticket types as you need, each with its own price, description and capacity — or leave it as a single price for simple events.

sections.heading: Draft, publish, cancel
sections.body: Build the event privately, publish when you're ready, mark it complete after, or cancel it at any point. A switch decides whether it shows on your public booking page; another holds tickets for staff approval before they confirm; another lets people buy without an existing client account.

sections.heading: Public event page, ready to share
sections.body: Every published event has its own clean public page with the cover image, description, schedule, capacity left and a tier picker. A copy-link button is one click away — paste straight into Instagram, WhatsApp or an email.

sections.heading: Tickets with QR codes
sections.body: Every ticket carries the attendee's name and contact, optional seat number, the price they paid, a unique ticket number and a QR code rendered ready to display. Each ticket has its own public URL, so the client can show it from their phone at the door.

sections.heading: Add a ticket by hand
sections.body: Staff can register a walk-in or someone who paid by bank transfer right from the tickets page — name, email, phone — and the system generates a fresh ticket number and QR for them on the spot.

sections.heading: Approval workflow when you need it
sections.body: Turn on approval for invitation-only events: new tickets start as Pending, an admin approves or rejects, and only approved attendees get the confirmation message and the QR code.

sections.heading: Reserved → confirmed → checked in
sections.body: Every ticket has a clear state — Reserved while payment is pending, Confirmed once paid, Checked-in on entry, or Cancelled with a reason or No-show after the event ends. The detail page counts each.

sections.heading: QR scan or manual check-in
sections.body: Open the camera and scan a ticket's QR to check the attendee in instantly — or pick them from a search list and confirm by name. Both flows record who let them in and when.

sections.heading: Live attendance at the door
sections.body: A live tablet-friendly screen shows tickets sold, attendees checked in, capacity headroom and the ten most recent check-ins with names and timestamps — so the host always knows how full the room is.

sections.heading: Waitlist with offers
sections.body: When a sold-out event has interest, queue people on the waitlist with their names, contact and how many seats they need. When a spot opens, notify the next person with a claim link that expires after a window you choose.

sections.heading: Twelve automatic messages
sections.body: Attendees get registration confirmations, 24-hour and 1-hour reminders (with the meeting URL for online events), updates if the schedule changes, cancellation notices with any refund amount, waitlist offers, and post-event check-in confirmations with optional certificate and feedback links. Staff get separate alerts when tickets are sold, capacity is reached, someone joins the waitlist or someone checks in.

sections.heading: Ticket revenue lands in your books
sections.body: Paid tickets become a sale automatically and ride the same revenue, tax and refund rails as everything else you sell — so your reports never have a separate "events" silo to reconcile.

sections.heading: Capacity guards and safe cancellation
sections.body: The system stops selling once the event or a tier hits its cap and pings staff that capacity is reached. Cancelling an event marks every active ticket as cancelled, captures the reason and notifies every attendee. Deleting an event requires confirmation and only works when no live ticket would be lost.

items.title: Team & HR
items.summary: From hire to payday — one place for shifts, attendance, leave, commissions and a payroll that posts to your books on its own.

sections.heading: Roster and invitations
sections.body: A searchable list of every team member with status, branch and date filters and a full export. "Send invite" emails the new hire a tokenised link that takes them straight to a join page — and the Owner is protected so no one can accidentally remove the boss.

sections.heading: One profile, six tabs
sections.body: Each member opens to Overview, Schedule, Finance, Permissions, Notifications and Reviews — pre-bound to that person. Set their photo, profile color, title, department and a switch for whether clients can book them online.

sections.heading: Roles, permissions and reusable templates
sections.body: Three roles (Owner, Admin, Staff) plus a granular checklist that says who can sell, refund, manage expenses, run payroll and so on. Save a permission set as a template and apply it to every new hire — no more rebuilding the same list.

sections.heading: Members across multiple branches
sections.body: Attach a member to one branch, two branches or all of them. The active-branch filter then restricts the calendar, schedules, attendance and pay runs to that branch — perfect for multi-location operators.

sections.heading: Compensation per member
sections.body: Set each person's pay type (hourly, salary or commission-only), pay frequency, hourly rate or monthly salary, and contract dates. The pay numbers are protected by a permission so only authorized roles can see them.

sections.heading: Default and per-service commission
sections.body: Set a member's default commission as a percentage or a flat amount, and override it for specific services — useful when a senior therapist takes a higher cut on premium services. Tips follow the rule you've chosen — passed through to staff, or recognised as revenue.

sections.heading: Weekly schedule with shifts and breaks
sections.body: Drag shifts and breaks into each staff member's day. Schedule changes can take effect on a future date so you can plan next month's rota without disturbing this one. Copy a day's shifts to other days in one click; approved leave and blocked time appear on the same grid.

sections.heading: Per-staff blocked time
sections.body: Block any slot for vacation, training, lunch or equipment maintenance — daily, weekly or monthly until an end date, with colored reasons. Edit the whole recurring series at once, and the calendar respects the same blocks.

sections.heading: Attendance with auto-close
sections.body: Staff clock in and out from the dashboard with multi-break tracking. The system records late arrivals and early departures against the day's scheduled shift, and any session left open more than 24 hours auto-closes to the scheduled end so timesheets stay clean.

sections.heading: Timesheet that's ready for payroll
sections.body: Per-member daily breakdown — scheduled hours vs actual hours, overtime, late arrivals — with totals for the date range. Export per member and feed the next pay run with the right hours by default.

sections.heading: Leave types, requests and balances
sections.body: Define your own leave types (paid or unpaid, color-coded, with optional annual quotas). Staff request leave, managers approve or reject with a note, and per-year balances track quota used, carried over and any adjustment — so balances never need reconstructing.

sections.heading: Pay runs that hit the books
sections.body: Build a pay run for the period and the branch you choose. Each entry tracks hours, overtime, base pay, commissions, bonuses, deductions and net pay — and when you mark it processed and then paid, the right journals land in your books automatically. Re-running won't double-post.

sections.heading: Staff reviews and replies
sections.body: Reviews left on completed appointments link to the staff member who delivered the service. Each member's profile shows their incoming reviews with the average rating, a reply box, and a switch to make a review public or private.

sections.heading: Each member mutes what they don't need
sections.body: Any team member can silence specific notifications on themselves without affecting their teammates — so the receptionist isn't pinged about pay runs and the accountant isn't pinged about every booking.

sections.heading: Impersonation for white-glove support
sections.body: Owners and admins can enter the dashboard as another team member, or sign into the client portal as a specific client, for hands-on support — without ever asking anyone for their password. Member invites and password resets are link-based and expire on their own.

sections.heading: Activity log per member
sections.body: Every sensitive action — sales, refunds, voids, expense approvals, supplier payments, pay-run processing, schedule edits, permission changes — is tied to the staff member who did it. "Who voided that ticket on Tuesday?" is a one-search answer.

items.title: Accounting
items.summary: A real double-entry ledger built into the same system you sell from — no second subscription, no monthly export ritual, no hand-off to an outside bookkeeper.

sections.heading: A real double-entry ledger
sections.body: Every transaction in the business posts a balanced journal entry — debits on one side, credits on the other — numbered, dated and traceable back to the receipt that caused it. Built for fast reports and an honest audit.

sections.heading: Chart of accounts you can adapt
sections.body: A complete chart of accounts ships out of the box — assets, liabilities, equity, revenue and expense — with parent-child grouping for unlimited depth. Rename or remap accounts to match your own conventions; the system codes that route automatic postings stay safe so nothing breaks behind the scenes.

sections.heading: Every operational event posts itself
sections.body: Sales, invoice payments, refunds, write-offs, credit notes, expenses, supplier payments, bills, gift card issuance and redemption, membership sales, deferred-revenue recognition, cash register open/close, depreciation, bad-debt provisioning, payroll and asset disposal — all land in the ledger automatically against the right contra account. You never type a manual journal to keep up.

sections.heading: Manual entries with one-click reverse
sections.body: Owners and admins can post manual entries for adjustments, opening balances and prior-period corrections. Every entry has a Reverse button that creates a mirrored entry — the original stays readable and the audit trail stays clean.

sections.heading: Tamper-evident audit history
sections.body: Every sensitive action is captured in an activity log that's cryptographically chained — each entry signs the one before it. A single tap re-verifies the whole chain and tells you exactly which row, if any, was tampered with. Evidence-grade history for the auditor, the regulator or the board.

sections.heading: Per-branch profit and loss
sections.body: Every entry can belong to a specific branch. Tenant-wide entries (rent, depreciation, opening balances) appear in every branch report. Run a branch P&L without losing the centrally-paid costs that genuinely belong to it.

sections.heading: Six built-in financial statements
sections.body: Balance sheet, income statement, cash flow, trial balance, general ledger and VAT return — each parameterised by date range and optional branch. The accounting overview runs two health checks live so you always know the books are balanced before you read them.

sections.heading: Aging for receivables and payables
sections.body: See what customers owe you and what you owe suppliers, broken into Current, 1–30, 31–60, 61–90 and 90+ day buckets. The same data feeds bad-debt provisioning, and per-customer and per-supplier statements are one tap away.

sections.heading: Bank reconciliation that won't fight you
sections.body: Add a bank account, import the statement, and the system suggests matches against the payments and expenses you've already recorded. Match, unmatch, ignore (for fees), and reconcile a period — every action is logged.

sections.heading: Bills and supplier payments
sections.body: Record vendor invoices for non-inventory costs (rent, utilities, marketing) with per-line account categorisation. On approval they post automatically against accounts payable; supplier payments draw the balance down and reconcile against the bank import in one tap.

sections.heading: Fixed assets and monthly depreciation
sections.body: Track equipment, furniture and vehicles with their cost, salvage value, useful life and depreciation method. The system depreciates them month-by-month for you, posts the entry, and handles disposals with the right gain or loss.

sections.heading: Memberships recognised properly
sections.body: Selling a year-long membership doesn't show as a year of revenue overnight. Earned portions move to revenue month by month over the membership window — IFRS 15 / ASC 606 compliant, with no spreadsheets.

sections.heading: Bad-debt provisioning automatically
sections.body: Each month the system trues up your Allowance for Doubtful Accounts against the aging buckets and posts the provision. Writing off an invoice books the expense against receivables — so the ledger always tells the truth.

sections.heading: Pending-refund approval
sections.body: Set a threshold above which refunds need a second pair of eyes. Refunds over the threshold queue as Pending — listed by requester, amount and reason — until a different team member approves or rejects. No money moves until they do.

sections.heading: Period close and opening balances
sections.body: Lock the books up to a chosen date so back-dated entries can never accidentally land in a closed month. When you bring an existing business onto the system, the opening-balances editor seeds the ledger live with a single balanced journal on the date you choose.

items.title: Reports
items.summary: Answer every "how are we doing?" in two clicks — with numbers you can defend to your accountant, your board or your bank.

sections.heading: Finance reports
sections.body: Finance: P&L, balance sheet, cash flow, trial balance and general ledger

sections.heading: Tax reports
sections.body: Tax: VAT return, AR/AP aging, period summaries and regulator-ready exports

sections.heading: Operations reports
sections.body: Operations: appointments, utilization, rebook rate and no-show analysis

sections.heading: People reports
sections.body: People: commissions, attendance, leave, timesheets and staff KPIs

sections.heading: Client reports
sections.body: Clients: cohort retention, lifetime value and segment-level performance

sections.heading: Inventory reports
sections.body: Inventory: valuation, movement, stock-vs-sales and low-stock forecasts

sections.heading: Add-on reports
sections.body: Specialty reports per add-on — prescriptions, lab orders, medical records, resource utilization

sections.heading: Date ranges & drill-down

items.a: Yes. Both plans include lifetime updates — every future release is yours at no additional cost. No renewal, no tier upgrade, no surprise bill.

items.q: What's the difference between Self-install and Done-for-you?
items.a: Exact same source code, same unlimited tenants, same lifetime updates. Self-install means you deploy it yourself (takes about an hour with our Docker Compose guide). Done-for-you means we deploy on your VPS, configure your tenant and branding, activate ZATCA/ETA, migrate your existing data, and run a 60-minute training session with your team.

items.q: Can I try before I buy?
items.a: Yes — a live demo URL is available from the hero and pricing page. Click through the full product with sample data before purchasing.

Primary pricing is in USD. At checkout you can pay in USD, SAR, EGP, EUR, GBP or AED — Lemon Squeezy auto-converts. Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Apple Pay and Google Pay are supported. For bank transfer on invoice, email support@momentfy.com.

items.q: Can I upgrade from Self-install to Done-for-you later?
items.a: Yes. Pay the difference and we'll schedule your installation. Just email with your order number.

items.q: Do you bill VAT or tax?
items.a: Lemon Squeezy handles VAT, MoR obligations, and country-specific tax registration — including Saudi 15% VAT and EU VAT — automatically at checkout.

items.q: Can I buy one license for multiple businesses I run?
items.a: One license covers one legal entity. If you operate two separate entities, you need two licenses. For multi-brand deployments inside a single entity, one license is fine.

All sales are final once the source is downloaded. If your download link fails or the archive is materially broken and we can't fix it within 7 days, we'll issue a full refund. See the Sales policy on the legal page.
